directive.title: Static RFQ dashboard with performance budget enforcement

auditor.summary: The generated code provides a solid foundation for the RFQ dashboard with proper TypeScript types, mock data covering all permutations, and performance monitoring infrastructure. However, there are critical buildability issues: the performance.ts file imports React hooks (useEffect) which will cause build failures when imported by Server Components. The PerformanceMonitor.mark() call in the server component page.tsx is architecturally incorrect. These issues must be resolved before the build can compile. The code structure otherwise follows the spec well with dynamic imports, skeleton loading, and proper formatting. Score is reduced due to build-breaking issues that prevent verification of acceptance criteria.

top_issues.description: Import of 'react' (useEffect) in a file that is used in server components (page.tsx imports PerformanceMonitor). This will cause a build error because 'react' cannot be imported in a module that is imported by a Server Component unless it's a Client Component boundary.

PerformanceMonitor.mark('dashboard-render-start') is called at the top of a Server Component. PerformanceMonitor.mark() checks typeof window, but the import of PerformanceMonitor itself may cause issues if it imports from 'react' (see above). Additionally, performance.mark() in a server component is meaningless.
The usePerformanceMark hook uses useEffect but the file is imported by server components. This violates Next.js 14 rules about importing hooks in server components.
